name: Puerto de Sabar and Axarquía Villages Loop
name_original: Puerto de Sabar – circular por los pueblos de Axarquía
description: Cycle the moderate 24.4-mile Puerto de Sabar loop through Axarquía's white villages, gaining 2441 feet with impressive mountain and sea view
region_intro: Road cycling around Riogordo offers diverse mountainous terrain, nestled between the Sierra de Camarolos and the Montes de Málaga. The region features varied landscapes, from elevated agricultural plateaus to rugged mountain environments and picturesque white villages. Cyclists encounter significant elevation gains across its routes, providing challenging climbs and scenic journeys through the Axarquía highlands. Best road cycling routes around Riogordo The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Sabar and Axarquía Villages Loop, a 24.4 miles (39.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through the mountainous landscape and traditional villages of the Axarquía region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Climb to Casabermeja – Queen's Fountain loop from Arroyo de Solano, a difficult 55.9 miles (90.0 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain, passing through the Montes de Málaga Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the Camino de Olías – Comares Village loop from Los Chamizos, a 53.8 miles (86.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and white villages, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling around Riogordo is defined by mountainous terrain, white villages, and varied landscapes, offering options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Riogordo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 2,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Riogordo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Riogordo?
A: Road cycling around Riogordo is characterized by its stunning mountainous terrain, nestled between the Sierra de Camarolos and the Montes de Málaga. You'll encounter varied landscapes, from elevated agricultural plateaus and subtropical farmlands to rugged mountain environments and picturesque white villages. Expect significant elevation gains, providing both challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant climbs, Riogordo offers options for various ability levels, including easy and moderate routes. The region has 59 road cycling routes in total, with 2 classified as easy and 28 as moderate, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling routes in the Riogordo area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Riogordo offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3954577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Casabermeja – Queen's Fountain loop from Arroyo de Solano&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 90 km path with over 2,200 meters of elevation gain, passing through the Montes de Málaga Natural Park. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14778863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Olías – Comares Village loop from Los Chamizos&lt;/a&gt;, covering 86.5 km with similar elevation.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes around Riogordo offer breathtaking natural beauty. You can encounter the pine forests and expansive viewpoints of the Montes de Málaga Natural Park. Notable natural monuments include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, the source of the Guadalhorce River, and the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Gomer and Doña Ana cliffs&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the tranquil Rio de la Cueva with its lush vegetation.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots recommended for cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer spectacular vistas. The Montes de Málaga Natural Park is renowned for its expansive viewpoints. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Gomer and Doña Ana cliffs&lt;/a&gt; provides a stunning panorama. The white villages themselves offer picturesque settings and unique photo opportunities.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Riogordo?
A: The road cycling routes in Riogordo are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse mountainous landscape, the challenging climbs, and the charm of the traditional white villages encountered along the way. Over 2,900 road cyclists have already explored this region using komoot.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Riogordo?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. These seasons provide ideal conditions for tackling the mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available around Riogordo?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Riogordo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938917838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Sabar and Axarquía Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route covering 39.3 km, navigating through mountainous landscapes and traditional villages.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes that pass through Riogordo?
A: Riogordo is a significant stop on longer cycling journeys. It's part of a two-day cycling route connecting the eastern coastline of Málaga with the Axarquía highlands, offering varied landscapes. It is also integrated into the Santiago de Compostela cycling route, blending sporting activity with cultural exploration for those undertaking longer tours.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or summits that road cyclists can aim to conquer in the area?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Riogordo offers several peaks and summits that can be incorporated into challenging rides. Notable peaks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4191171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Vilo&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6323364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7139727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo Gomer Summit&lt;/a&gt;. These offer rewarding climbs and often spectacular views from the top.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or other water bodies?
A: Yes, some routes in the region will take you past significant water bodies. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40644217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benamargosa – La Viñuela Reservoir loop from Riogordo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 99 km route that includes views of the beautiful La Viñuela Reservoir, offering a different scenic element to your ride.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on road cycling routes around Riogordo?
A: Given the mountainous nature of the region, you can expect significant elevation gains. Many routes feature climbs well over 1,000 meters. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3954577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Casabermeja – Queen's Fountain loop from Arroyo de Solano&lt;/a&gt; has over 2,200 meters of ascent, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17234474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Sábar – Puerto del Sol (1085 m) loop from Riogordo&lt;/a&gt; features over 2,000 meters of climbing.
tips: Riogordo is a town with an Arab past, visible in its white houses and steep alleys. This town is part of the Oil Route and the Axarquia Mountains.
Colmenar, called a town with a taste of honey. The name comes from the hives of bees, since beekeeping was the basis of the local economy for a long time.

This mountain pass is located 590 meters above sea level. Here you can start the route of the Tajos de Sábar de Alfarnatejo; On this tour you will visit the Doña Ana pit, the Gomer pit and the Fraile pit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Puerto de Sabar circular route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is rated as moderate and typically takes around 2 hours and 6 minutes of active riding time. However, including stops to enjoy the scenery and villages, the total experience can span up to 5 hours.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Puerto de Sabar circular route is rated as moderate. It features winding roads with consistently steep gradients, characteristic of the mountainous Axarquía region, and requires a good level of fitness. While mostly well-paved, some segments may be unpaved and more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, so you can typically start and park in one of the villages along the route, such as Riogordo or Colmenar. Specific parking areas are usually available within these settlements.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto de Sabar circular route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating and consistently steep gradients, this route is generally more suitable for intermediate to advanced riders with a good level of fitness. Beginners might find the elevation gain and some unpaved segments challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly well-paved roads, ideal for racebikes. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved and more challenging. The route features winding roads with consistently steep gradients as you navigate through the mountainous Axarquía region, offering diverse landscapes from olive groves to pine-scented mountains.

faqs.question: What are the scenic highlights along the Puerto de Sabar circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers tranquil roads through traditional white villages like Riogordo, Colmenar, and Alfarnatejo. You'll also pass through the dramatic mountain pass of Puerto de Sábar, enjoy breathtaking sea views, and see vibrant flowers, olive trees, almond groves, and fruit orchards.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Axarquía region boasts over 300 days of sunshine annually, making it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for a racebike tour, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several charming white villages such as Riogordo, Colmenar, and Alfarnatejo, you will find cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and refresh.

faqs.question: Is the Puerto de Sabar circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not specifically designed as a dog-friendly trail. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured and consider the nature of road cycling for their comfort and safety.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Puerto de Sabar circular route. It utilizes public roads and passes through open landscapes and villages.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of official trails such as Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Málaga) and Pulgarín - Alfarnate, among others. This offers potential for extending or linking your ride with other local paths.
